snippets.title: List, dict and set comprehensions
snippets.code:
words = ["Gen", "AI", "gen", "ai"]

lower = [w.lower() for w in words]          # ['gen', 'ai', 'gen', 'ai']
lengths = {w: len(w) for w in words}        # {'Gen': 3, 'AI': 2, ...}
initials = {w[0].lower() for w in words}    # {'g', 'a'}

print(lower)
print(lengths)
print(initials)
snippets.explanation: Same idea, three containers: list keeps order/dupes, dict maps keys to values, set keeps unique items.

snippets.title: Filtering and per-item branching
snippets.code:
temps = [0.2, 0.9, 0.5, 0.8]

hot = [t for t in temps if t > 0.7]                 # filter
labels = ["hot" if t > 0.7 else "cold" for t in temps]   # transform

print(hot)      # [0.9, 0.8]
print(labels)   # ['cold', 'hot', 'cold', 'hot']
snippets.explanation: The trailing if filters items; a conditional expression in front chooses a value per item.

snippets.title: Generator expression for large/streamed data
snippets.code:
docs = ["gen ai rocks", "hello world", "retrieval augmented generation"]

# Lazy: no intermediate list is built
total_tokens = sum(len(d.split()) for d in docs)
long_docs = (d for d in docs if len(d.split()) >= 3)

print(total_tokens)        # 8
print(next(long_docs))     # 'gen ai rocks'
snippets.explanation: Parentheses create a generator expression that yields items on demand, saving memory.

snippets.title: Building LLM message payloads
snippets.code:
questions = ["What is RAG?", "What is a vector DB?"]
messages = [{"role": "user", "content": q} for q in questions]

chunks = [{"text": "a", "score": 0.91}, {"text": "b", "score": 0.42}]
kept = [c["text"] for c in chunks if c["score"] >= 0.8]

print(messages)
print(kept)   # ['a']
snippets.explanation: Comprehensions concisely shape API payloads and filter retrieved chunks by score.
